operation: First, a workplane is initialized on the XY coordinate plane. The sketch origin is moved to the (2, 2) coordinate point, then a 4 unit wide by 6 unit tall rectangle is sketched. This 2D shape is extruded 3 units along the positive Z axis to form a rectangular prism. Next, the topmost face aligned with the positive Z direction is selected, and a new workplane is created centered on this face. A circle with 1 unit radius is sketched at the workplane origin, then a blind cut of 1 unit depth in the negative Z direction is performed to create a shallow recessed hole in the top of the prism.
geometry: The model is a solid rectangular cuboid with flat vertical side faces and parallel horizontal top and bottom faces. A single shallow cylindrical blind hole is recessed into the center of the cuboid's top face, extending only partway through the total height of the block, with a diameter smaller than the shorter side length of the top rectangular face.
function: This is a simple alignment block or spacer with an integrated locating recess designed to seat a cylindrical pin or fastener, enabling precise positioning of mating components during assembly.
